In addition to thorough harmonic research and analysis, Care Lab s’ electrical installation services also include safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification. To make sure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and thorough, trained professionals will evaluate harmonic threats, training programs , documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE). Care Lab s can identify harmonics early on and take the required steps to lessen their impact, ensuring the equipment’s safety. Inefficiency in the electrical system may be caused by harmonic voltages and currents. They can lower the quality of the power and produce extraneous heat. A mathematical method for determining potential resonances and levels of harmonic distortion is called harmonic voltage analysis. Based on gathered data, harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical method for identifying potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Harmonic frequencies are added to the electrical system by nonlinear electrical switching equipment such AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, computers, and so on. When harmonic voltage/current levels are high, a lot of equipment can fail. As a result, harmonic analysis and study must be done as soon as possible. Currently, there are 5% harmonic limits in 415 voltage systems, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics.

Harmonic problems are incredibly rare, but when they do happen, they significantly affect the reliability, power quality, and efficiency of the power system. Thus, harmonic research and analysis are crucial.

By shedding light on the causes and implications of power quality problems, harmonic research and analysis can help designers avoid harmonic-related challenges and lessen the chance of unexpected outcomes.
The Stage 2 values are higher if background harmonics have already been detected.
